name: carbon_market
description: A carbon cap-and-trade market: a regulated firm emits CO2, then buys, sells, and retires allowances on a price-responsive market to stay within its emissions cap.

card.description: A carbon cap-and-trade market: a regulated firm emits CO2, then buys, sells, and retires allowances on a price-responsive market to stay within its emissions cap.

rules: 'emit' releases one tonne of CO2 (times the optional 'qty'): emissions rises and the carbon price drifts up as compliance demand tightens.

'buy_credit' purchases one allowance per unit from the market at the current price while market_supply exists and cash covers it: credits +1, cash -price, market_supply -1, and the price rises with demand.
'sell_credit' sells one held allowance per unit back to the market: credits -1, cash +price, market_supply +1, and the price falls (never below 1.0).
'retire' permanently surrenders one held allowance per unit for compliance: credits -1, retired +1; retired allowances never re-enter the market.
Trades that cannot be afforded or covered are skipped; no balance ever goes negative.
A firm is compliant only when every emitted tonne is covered by a retired allowance (uncovered == 0) and cumulative emissions stay within the cap (over_cap == 0).

emit.report: emitted {emissions}t, retired {retired}, holding {credits} allowances; cash {cash} at price {price}; {uncovered} tonnes uncovered (compliant={compliant})
